talk_metadata.session_title: From Molecular Mechanisms to Therapeutic Opportunity in Prostate Cancer
talk_metadata.talk_title: Heterobifunctional reprogramming of androgen receptor complexes in prostate cancer (DALTACs)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we have not yet generated enzalutamide‑resistant PDX organoids
Speaker said: we have not done normal tissue toxicity studies yet

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: DALTAC heterobifunctional molecules targeting AR and p300/CBP
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Efficacy appears limited to AR‑positive, p300‑dependent prostate cancer cells; AR‑negative or non‑prostate lineages are insensitive
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Relies on AR positivity and high p300 expression, potentially diluting signal in heterogeneous patient populations

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that DELTACs increase AR chromatin recruitment but concluded that the compounds act as a ‘molecular monkey wrench’ to inhibit AR signaling, despite the apparent AR activation

key_data_gaps: No in vivo toxicity data in normal androgen‑dependent tissues
No efficacy data in enzalutamide‑resistant patient‑derived organoids

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify tumor subsets with sufficient AR and p300/CBP expression to predict DELTAC sensitivity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: the levels of AR and p300 are elevated... we are using models where AR is often amplified
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate transcriptomic and proteomic data to define quantitative thresholds for AR/p300 co‑dependency

crispro_opportunity.description: Predict emergence of antigen loss or compensatory pathways (e.g., BRD4 displacement) that may confer resistance to DALTACs
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted loss of BRD4 and Pol II at AR sites after DELTAC treatment
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model network rewiring after domain alteration to anticipate compensatory dependencies

cited_competitors.name: Arvinas
cited_competitors.context: Developed AR PROTAC degrader used as a reference for heterobifunctional strategies

cited_competitors.name: Cellcentrix
cited_competitors.context: Developed p300/CBP bromodomain inhibitor that was paused due to tolerability

cited_competitors.name: Halda
cited_competitors.context: Created AR‑BRD4 heterobifunctional compound referenced as a parallel approach

unresolved_questions: Will DELTACs be safe in normal androgen‑dependent tissues such as seminal vesicle?
Can DELTACs overcome resistance mechanisms in enzalutamide‑resistant organoid models?

talk_metadata.session_title: From Molecular Mechanisms to Therapeutic Opportunity in Prostate Cancer
talk_metadata.talk_title: Emerging therapeutic strategies in advanced prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: the drug has not reached MTD and is very well tolerated
Speaker said: we are confident the AR‑BRD4 glue will work even without castration

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited activity in AR splice‑variant disease

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Simultaneous disruption of AR and BRD4 transcriptional complexes
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling heavily pre‑treated CRPC without biomarker selection for AR/BRD4 dependency
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: AR splice‑variant status or BRD4 expression levels
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small sample size, no control arm, risk of over‑interpreting PSA declines

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: gocatumig DLL3 T‑cell engager phase I/II
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: DLL3‑directed T‑cell engagement in neuroendocrine prostate cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No pre‑selection based on quantitative DLL3 expression despite heterogeneous antigen levels

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Compound also engages BRD2/3, raising potential off‑target effects
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad label without molecular selection may dilute efficacy signals

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Masked constructs still generate anti‑drug antibodies and CRS in some patients
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Requires careful dosing schedules to balance efficacy and CRS

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented deep tumor regressions with the AR‑BRD4 glue yet concluded that the drug does not need to block AR signaling, despite data showing increased AR chromatin binding

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine AR‑BRD4 glue with PARP inhibition to exploit reported myeloid clearance and enhance response
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned a paper under review showing PARP inhibition clears the myeloid compartment
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic interactions between chromatin‑targeting glues and DNA‑damage response inhibitors

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop quantitative DLL3 thresholds to select patients for gocatumig therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted lack of strong association between DLL3 levels and response in the trial
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate IHC, transcriptomics, and spatial data to define predictive DLL3 expression cutoffs

cited_competitors.name: BMS AR degrader
cited_competitors.context: Discussed as the most promising AR degrader with activity in AR‑mutant disease

cited_competitors.name: Janssen PSMA CD3 engager
cited_competitors.context: Presented data on deep PSA declines and tumor regressions

cited_competitors.name: AstraZeneca STEAP1/2 programs
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as parallel cell‑surface targeting approaches

unresolved_questions: Will the AR‑BRD4 glue retain activity in hormone‑sensitive metastatic disease without castration?
What is the optimal biomarker (AR splice‑variant, BRD4 expression) to enrich for responders?

talk_metadata.session_title: From Molecular Mechanisms to Therapeutic Opportunity in Prostate Cancer
talk_metadata.talk_title: Therapeutic advances in neuroendocrine prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: DLL3 expression did not strongly correlate with response in our trial
Speaker said: EZH2 inhibition in neuroendocrine models actually pushes tumors toward a more neuroendocrine state

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Heterogeneous intra‑tumoral DLL3 expression leading to antigen loss after treatment
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: we see overall lower levels of antigen suggesting a potential acquired resistance mechanism
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker said: response rates were approaching 50% despite variable DLL3 levels

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: EZH2 inhibition up‑regulates neurogenesis genes and may drive further neuroendocrine differentiation

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: gocatumig DLL3 T‑cell engager phase I/II
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: DLL3‑mediated T‑cell redirection in neuroendocrine prostate cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled patients based on histology without quantitative DLL3 enrichment
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Standardized DLL3 IHC cutoff or PET imaging for antigen burden
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial monitoring of DLL3 loss during therapy

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Efficacy appears independent of DLL3 expression level, indicating antigen heterogeneity limits therapeutic window
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Lack of robust companion diagnostic to select high‑DLL3 patients

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: May exacerbate neuroendocrine phenotype rather than reverse it
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Unclear patient subset that would benefit; risk of promoting terminal differentiation

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that DLL3 expression is heterogeneous and not predictive of response yet concluded that DLL3‑targeted agents are promising for NEPC

key_data_gaps: Lack of correlation between DLL3 IHC/PET and clinical outcomes
No long‑term OS data for EZH2 inhibitor combinations

crispro_opportunity.description: Create a CrisPRO‑driven composite biomarker integrating DLL3 IHC, PET imaging, and spatial transcriptomics to predict responder status
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: DLL3 expression did not strongly correlate with response in our trial
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model spatial heterogeneity and predict antigen loss dynamics

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to identify tumors where EZH2 inhibition will drive neuroendocrine gene up‑regulation and avoid those patients
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: EZH2 inhibition in neuroendocrine models up‑regulated neuronal transcription factors
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict transcriptional rewiring after EZH2 blockade to flag high‑risk cases

crispro_opportunity.description: Pair DLL3 T‑cell engagers with agents that prevent antigen loss (e.g., epigenetic modulators) identified by CrisPRO simulations
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted acquired resistance via lower DLL3 levels after treatment
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate combination effects on DLL3 expression stability

cited_competitors.name: AstraZeneca STEAP2 programs
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as parallel cell‑surface targeting approaches

cited_competitors.name: Janssen KLK2 CD3 engager
cited_competitors.context: Cited as another successful T‑cell engager platform

unresolved_questions: What molecular features (beyond DLL3) predict durable responses to DLL3‑directed T‑cell engagers?
Can EZH2 inhibition be combined with other epigenetic agents to avoid forward neuroendocrine differentiation?
